evals.prompt: make this error handling code beautiful
evals.expected_output: Student executes the task (rewrites error handling), then Socrates convenes a dialectic of philosophers who review in rounds. Work only passes when philosophers reach consensus. Dialectic transcripts written under /tmp/student-counsel-*/dialectic/.
expectations: Student produces a complete rewrite of the error handling — not bullet points or suggestions
Socrates is spawned as a subagent to convene the dialectic after student work is complete
At least Socrates and Aristotle (both philosopher subagents) are spawned for review
Dialectic transcripts are written to /tmp/student-counsel-*/dialectic/dialectic-round-N.md
Final output includes the north star sentence ('The Form sought: ...') and round count
DONE: The work is beautiful or DONE: AS CLOSE TO THE FORM AS MORTAL HANDS CAN ACHIEVE

evals.prompt: student-counsel: design a CLI interface for a task runner — make it perfect
evals.expected_output: Student designs the CLI, dialectic reviews with design-specific beauty criteria (necessity, visual hierarchy, element justification).
expectations: Triggers on 'student-counsel' and 'perfect' phrasing
Student reads beauty-criteria.md before beginning work
Student states a north star sentence before producing work
Dialectic evaluates against the stated Form — not generic quality
Revision requests from philosophers are specific and actionable

evals.prompt: fix the null pointer in src/auth.ts — it's urgent, production is down right now
evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger student-counsel. Speed is the explicit priority — bug is fixed directly without dialectic.
expectations: Student-counsel is NOT triggered
No north star sentence or Form language appears
No dialectic directory is created in the workspace
No philosopher subagents are spawned
Bug is fixed directly with minimal ceremony

evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger. Factual question with no beauty-seeking or refinement intent.
expectations: Student-counsel is NOT triggered
No philosopher subagents or dialectic rounds
Response is a short factual answer
No reference to Socrates, Aristotle, Plotinus, or Diotima

evals.prompt: Fix a missing comma in a JSON config file.
evals.expected_output: Takes the lite tier: fixes directly with a self-check; skips the dialectic council entirely.
expectations: Takes the lite tier — does not convene Socrates/Aristotle for a missing comma
Applies the fix directly
